sections.subtitle: Match Overview and League Standings
sections.text: Real Betis, sitting in 6th place with 57 points, host Osasuna, who are positioned 11th with 44 points, at the Benito Villamarin in Seville. This matchup promises to be intriguing as Betis aim to solidify their strong standing in the league. Osasuna, on the other hand, will look to climb the table with an upset. The difference of 13 points highlights Betis’ dominance this season. Both teams have had mixed results recently, but Betis’ home advantage could play a significant role.

sections.subtitle: Historical Context and Head-to-Head
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two sides, Real Betis have a clear edge with 4 wins compared to Osasuna’s 1 win. In their most recent encounter on October 19, 2024, Betis secured a 2-1 victory away at Osasuna, with Chimy Ávila earning the Player of the Match title with a 7.9 Sofascore rating. Head-to-head trends show Betis have been first to score in 9 out of the last 10 meetings. Additionally, both teams have scored in 4 of the last 5 encounters, suggesting we could see goals from both sides again. Betis also have a strong record as first-half winners in 5 of the last 7 games against Osasuna.

sections.subtitle: Real Betis Key Players and Form
sections.text: Real Betis boast several in-form players, including Isco, who ranks 5th in the league with an average Sofascore rating of 7.56, scoring 8 goals and providing 6 assists this season. Giovani Lo Celso stands out as well, with 8 goals and an impressive 2.7 fouls drawn per game, ranking 1st in the league for the latter. Antony has been exceptional recently, averaging an 8.12 rating over the last 5 matches with 4 goals and 1 assist. Other players to watch include Cucho Hernández, who has contributed 3 goals, and Abdessamad Ezzalzouli, with 3 goals in his last 5 games. Betis’ squad depth and attacking flair, with 52 goals scored this season (6th in the league), make them a formidable force.

sections.subtitle: Osasuna Key Players and Form
sections.text: Osasuna will rely heavily on Ante Budimir, who ranks 3rd in the league for goals scored with 18, complemented by 4 assists and a recent form of 3 goals in 5 matches. Rubén García is another standout, with 5 goals and 5 assists, and an average rating of 7.58 over the last 5 games. Aimar Oroz also adds value in midfield with 5 goals this season. While Osasuna have struggled for consistency, their attacking output could pose a threat, especially with Budimir leading the line. Players like Lucas Torró, with 3 goals, will need to step up to support the attack against a strong Betis defense.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Team Statistics
sections.text: Real Betis have been dominant in several statistical categories, ranking 6th in total goals scored (52), 6th in average ball possession (52.6%), and 1st in interceptions per game (9.47). Their ability to create big chances (81, ranked 6th) and win possessions in the final third (4.44 per game, ranked 3rd) highlights their pressing game. Osasuna, however, have shown resilience, with a notable 24% of their goals coming in the 61-75 and 76-90 minute periods, indicating late-game threats. Betis’ recent streak of scoring first in 7 of their last 8 home games could set the tone early. Both teams struggle with clean sheets, with Betis failing to keep one in their last 7 home matches and Osasuna in their last 11 away games against Betis.

sections.subtitle: Referee and Disciplinary Expectations
sections.text: The match will be officiated by Miguel Angel Ortiz Arias, who averages 4.6 yellow cards and 0.33 red cards per game. Given Betis’ record of 67 yellow cards this season (4th least in the league) and a head-to-head trend of less than 4.5 cards in 7 of the last 9 matches between these teams, we might see a relatively disciplined game. However, with both teams known for competitive play, a few bookings are still likely. Osasuna’s physical approach could test the referee’s patience, especially in a high-stakes environment at Benito Villamarin.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players and Squad Impact
sections.text: Real Betis will be without key players like Diego Llorente, Chimy Ávila, and Marc Roca, which could impact their defensive stability and attacking options. Osasuna, meanwhile, miss Raúl García de Haro and Jon Moncayola, potentially weakening their forward line and midfield creativity. These absences might tilt the balance slightly in Betis’ favor, given their deeper squad. However, Osasuna will still rely on their in-form stars to compensate. The impact of missing players could be crucial in tight moments of the game.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Streaks
sections.text: Real Betis are on a 6-game streak without a loss at home, a promising sign for their fans. They’ve also seen over 2.5 goals in their last 5 home matches, with both teams scoring in all 7 of their recent home games. Osasuna have managed to score in 4 of their last 5 away games against Betis, showing they can find the net on the road. Betis rank 3rd in the league for headers scored (11), which could be a key weapon against Osasuna’s defense. Additionally, Betis average 5.2 corners per game (ranked 4th), suggesting they might dominate set-piece opportunities.
